body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: Communication from the School of Communication to parents regarding student progress is decent and fulfills the minimum requirements, but falls short of what a genuinely family-supportive institution should provide. The parent portal provides access to basic grade and attendance data, but the interface is somewhat outdated and not particularly intuitive for parents who are not technology-confident. Proactive outreach — for example, early alerts when a student's performance or attendance begins to decline — is currently limited and reactive. More frequent, personalized, and timely communication from the Academic Affairs Office would significantly strengthen the partnership between parents and the institution in supporting student success.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: The teaching quality in my Public Relations courses at the School of Communication has been exceptional this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly praise the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: Career preparation services at the School of Communication, offered through the Career Center, are moderate and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: Campus safety standards at the School of Communication are moderate, providing an adequate baseline of physical security and student welfare programming while leaving meaningful room for improvement in several critical areas. Core security measures — including campus access controls, security patrols, and emergency contact systems — are functional and reasonably communicated. However, mental health and counseling resources are stretched and insufficiently staffed relative to the student population, creating unacceptable wait times during periods of peak demand. I would strongly encourage the Student Affairs Office to expand counseling capacity, improve lighting and surveillance in underserved campus areas, and develop a more proactive student welfare outreach program for at-risk individuals.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: I have experienced problematic financial management from the Finance Department at the School of Communication that has caused considerable stress and inconvenience to our family over the course of this academic year. Scholarship disbursements were repeatedly delayed without advance notification, and when I contacted the Finance Department for clarification, responses were slow and sometimes contradictory between staff members. Unexpected additional charges appeared on the fee statement mid-semester without prior written notice, which I find both financially disruptive and contrary to good institutional governance practices. I formally request that the Finance Department implement automated disbursement notifications, enforce accurate billing practices, and establish a clear parent-facing communication protocol for all financial matters.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal at the School of Communication, managed by the IT Support Department, is substandard in its functionality, reliability, and user experience, and has been more of a source of frustration than a useful communication tool. The interface is dated and difficult to navigate, with key information such as grade details and attendance records buried under multiple menu layers. The system experiences frequent downtime — often during critical periods such as grade release and enrollment — and error messages provide no useful guidance for resolving access issues independently. I strongly suggest a complete redesign of the parent portal with a focus on intuitive UX, mobile compatibility, and real-time data synchronization to bring it in line with modern digital standards.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling at the School of Communication is satisfactory, covering essential advising needs without providing the depth and personalization that would truly distinguish the service. My child has found their assigned advisor to be approachable and knowledgeable on the whole, though appointment availability is sometimes limited during peak periods of the academic calendar. The counseling provided tends to be reactive — responding to student-initiated queries rather than proactively identifying and addressing potential challenges before they escalate. A more structured check-in model, combined with a larger and better-trained advising team, would significantly improve the consistency and impact of academic counseling across the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: The student support services offered by the Student Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been remarkable and have made a genuine difference to my overall well-being and university experience throughout the semester. Counseling services are readily accessible, professionally delivered, and clearly communicated to students through multiple channels including the student portal, orientation sessions, and notice boards. The range of support available — from academic counseling to mental health resources and financial hardship assistance — reflects a comprehensive understanding of the diverse challenges students face during their academic journey. I also commend the regular student welfare check-ins organized by the office, which foster a sense of community and belonging on campus.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: The physical campus environment and facilities managed by the Facilities Management provide a exceptional learning atmosphere that I believe genuinely contributes to academic productivity and student well-being at the School of Communication. Lecture halls are spacious, well-ventilated, and equipped with modern audio-visual technology that facilitates clear and engaging presentations. Study spaces — including individual carrels, group collaboration rooms, and outdoor relaxation areas — are thoughtfully designed and well-maintained throughout the academic week. Cleaning and security standards are consistently high, and campus maintenance requests are typically resolved within a reasonable timeframe. The overall physical environment reflects a commitment to providing students with a safe, comfortable, and inspiring place to learn.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: My overall assessment of the School of Communication as a parent is that it offers a decent educational experience with genuine strengths that are unfortunately balanced by structural weaknesses that need sustained attention. The academic programs are broadly well-designed, and there are individual faculty members and support staff who clearly go above and beyond for students. However, system-level inconsistencies in service quality, communication, and resource provision create an uneven experience that depends too heavily on which individual staff members a student encounters. I encourage the institution to invest in building reliable systems and consistent standards that ensure every student — regardless of program or year — receives the same high-quality support and educational experience.
